Overview

Cherry Studio is a cross-platform desktop application designed as a unified client for interacting with over 50 Large Language Model (LLM) providers. It provides a professional-grade interface for AI assistants, autonomous agents, and knowledge base management, built on a modern Electron and React stack.

Purpose and Scope

Cherry Studio aims to be a "powerful AI assistant for producers" package.json5 It consolidates fragmented AI services into a single desktop environment, removing the need for users to manage multiple web tabs or complex CLI configurations.

The application is identified as CherryStudio (app ID com.kangfenmao.CherryStudio) electron-builder.yml1-2 It supports a wide range of use cases from simple chat to complex RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) and autonomous agent workflows.

Licensing

Cherry Studio operates under a dual-licensing model:

  • Community Edition: Licensed under GNU Affero General Public License v3.0 (AGPL-3.0) LICENSE1-2
  • Commercial/Enterprise Edition: Requires a commercial license for proprietary use, offering centralized management, shared knowledge bases, and enterprise backup README.md53

Contact: support@cherry-ai.com package.json8

High-Level Architecture

Cherry Studio follows the Electron multi-process architecture, separating the privileged Node.js environment from the user interface.

System Architecture Diagram

This diagram illustrates the data flow and process separation, mapping conceptual services to specific code implementation areas.

Sources: package.json7-95 electron.vite.config.ts20-125 src/main/ipc.ts44-94

Technology Stack

The project utilizes a monorepo-style structure with a focus on type safety and high-performance local data handling.

Technology to Code Entity Mapping

This diagram bridges "Natural Language Space" (the technologies used) to "Code Entity Space" (the specific files and classes).

Key Frameworks:

  • Electron: Core runtime for desktop integration package.json7
  • React 19: Frontend UI framework package.json121
  • Express 5.1.0: Powers the OpenAI-compatible local API server package.json95
  • Drizzle ORM & LibSQL: High-performance persistence for the Agent system package.json35-90
  • i18next: Localization system supporting 14+ languages including zh-CN, en-US, ja, and ru electron-builder.yml3-12

Sources: package.json7-121 electron-builder.yml3-12 src/main/ipc.ts44-50

Core Data Entities

The application's logic is structured around several primary TypeScript interfaces and services.

Entity Relationship Diagram

This diagram defines how the core data structures interact within the application.

Key Systems:

  • Assistant System: Pre-configured AI personas defined by the Assistant type src/renderer/src/types/index.ts34-64
  • Agent System: Includes the CherryClaw autonomous agent with personality systems (soul), scheduled tasks, and filesystem sandboxing electron-builder.yml151
  • Reasoning Models: Sophisticated handling of thinking models via ThinkingModelType and ReasoningEffortOption src/renderer/src/types/index.ts98-152
  • MCP (Model Context Protocol): Integration for external tools and resources via MCPService and MCPServer configurations src/renderer/src/types/index.ts16-56 src/main/ipc.ts60

Sources: src/renderer/src/types/index.ts34-152 electron-builder.yml144-158 src/main/ipc.ts60

Supported Platforms

Cherry Studio is built to be truly cross-platform, with specific build targets defined in the project configuration.

PlatformFormatsArchitectures
WindowsNSIS Installer, Portablex64, arm64 electron-builder.yml23-82
macOSDMG, ZIParm64, x64 electron-builder.yml26-110
LinuxAppImage, DEB, RPMx64, arm64 electron-builder.yml29-119

The application also registers the cherrystudio:// custom protocol for deep-linking electron-builder.yml16-19

Sources: electron-builder.yml16-119 package.json23-31
